mission.title: Install and Configure Strimzi Kafka Operator
mission.description: Strimzi Kafka Operator allows you to run Apache Kafka clusters on Kubernetes or OpenShift, providing a simple way to manage Kafka instances and their configurations. It's ideal for organizations looking to leverage Kafka's messaging capabilities in a cloud-native environment.

steps.title: Install Strimzi Kafka Operator
steps.description:
Use Helm to install the Strimzi Kafka Operator. Make sure you have Helm installed and configured. Run the following commands:

```bash
# Add the Strimzi Helm repository
helm repo add strimzi https://strimzi.io/charts
helm repo update

# Install the Strimzi Kafka Operator (version 0.44)
helm install strimzi-kafka-operator strimzi/strimzi-kafka-operator --version 0.44.0 --namespace kafka --create-namespace
```

Verify the installation:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kafka
```

steps.title: Create a Kafka Cluster
steps.description:
Create a Kafka cluster by applying a custom resource definition (CRD). Create a file named `kafka-cluster.yaml` with the following content:

```yaml
apiVersion: kafka.strimzi.io/v1beta2
kind: Kafka
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: kafka
spec:
  kafka:
    version: "3.4.0"
    replicas: 3
    listeners:
      plain:
        authentication:
          type: tls
      tls:
        authentication:
          type: tls
    storage:
      type: persistent-claim
      size: 10Gi
      class: standard
  zookeeper:
    replicas: 3
    storage:
      type: persistent-claim
      size: 10Gi
      class: standard
```

Apply the configuration:
```bash
kubectl apply -f kafka-cluster.yaml
```

Verify the Kafka cluster:
```bash
kubectl get kafka -n kafka
```

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Configure Kafka to enable monitoring and autoscaling. For monitoring, you can integrate with Prometheus and Grafana. To enable autoscaling, modify the Kafka CR:

```yaml
spec:
  kafka:
    ...
    metrics:
      enabled: true
    autoscaling:
      enabled: true
      minReplicas: 3
      maxReplicas: 10
```

Apply the updated configuration:
```bash
kubectl apply -f kafka-cluster.yaml
```

uninstall.title: Uninstall Strimzi Kafka Operator
uninstall.description:
To uninstall the Strimzi Kafka Operator and clean up resources, run the following commands:

```bash
# Delete the Kafka cluster
kubectl delete kafka my-cluster -n kafka

# Uninstall the Helm release
helm uninstall strimzi-kafka-operator -n kafka

# Optionally, delete the namespace
kubectl delete namespace kafka
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Strimzi Kafka Operator
upgrade.description:
To upgrade the Strimzi Kafka Operator from version 0.43 to 0.44, follow these steps:

1. Backup your current Kafka cluster configuration:
   ```bash
   kubectl get kafka my-cluster -n kafka -o yaml > kafka-backup.yaml
   ```
2. Upgrade the Helm release:
   ```bash
   helm upgrade strimzi-kafka-operator strimzi/strimzi-kafka-operator --version 0.44.0 -n kafka
   ```
3. Verify the upgrade:
   ```bash
   kubectl get pods -n kafka
   ```
4. If issues arise, rollback to the previous version:
   ```bash
   helm rollback strimzi-kafka-operator -n kafka
   ```

troubleshooting.title: Kafka Pod CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter a CrashLoopBackOff error with Kafka pods, check the logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kafka
```
Common issues include misconfiguration in the Kafka CR or insufficient resources. Ensure your storage class is correctly set up.

troubleshooting.title: Zookeeper Not Ready
troubleshooting.description:
If Zookeeper pods are not in a ready state, check the pod status:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kafka
```
Ensure that the storage class allows for persistent volumes and that the Zookeeper configuration in the Kafka CR is correct.

troubleshooting.title: Helm Upgrade Fails
troubleshooting.description:
If the Helm upgrade fails, check for existing resources that may conflict with the new version. Use:
```bash
helm history strimzi-kafka-operator -n kafka
```
You may need to manually delete conflicting resources before retrying the upgrade.

versionNotes.changes: Improved metrics support and enhanced autoscaling features.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for Kafka versions below 2.8.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for Kafka 3.3 and improved Helm chart configurations.

resolution.summary: A successful setup of the Strimzi Kafka Operator includes a running Kafka cluster with all pods in the 'Running' state and the ability to produce and consume messages. Monitoring and autoscaling should also be configured correctly.
codeSnippets:
# Add the Strimzi Helm repository
helm repo add strimzi https://strimzi.io/charts
helm repo update

# Install the Strimzi Kafka Operator (version 0.44)
helm install strimzi-kafka-operator strimzi/strimzi-kafka-operator --version 0.44.0 --namespace kafka --create-namespace

apiVersion: kafka.strimzi.io/v1beta2
kind: Kafka
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: kafka
spec:
  kafka:
    version: "3.4.0"
    replicas: 3
    listeners:
      plain:
        authentication:
          type: tls
      tls:
        authentication:
          type: tls
    storage:
      type: persistent-claim
      size: 10Gi
      class: standard
  zookeeper:
    replicas: 3
    storage:
      type: persistent-claim
      size: 10Gi
      class: standard

spec:
  kafka:
    ...
    metrics:
      enabled: true
    autoscaling:
      enabled: true
      minReplicas: 3
      maxReplicas: 10

prerequisites.cloudCLI: optional cloud CLI tool (gcloud, aws, az, oci)
prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and kubectl configured to interact with it.
